canonical_definition: A series of classic social psychology experiments studying how individual judgment is influenced by a majority group opinion, which inspired the design of the Trust and Doubt protocols.
papers: Do as We Do, Not as You Think: the Conformity of Large Language Models
Human Simulacra: Benchmarking the Personification of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A series of classic social psychology experiments studying how individual judgment is influenced by a majority group opinion, which inspired the design of the Trust and Doubt protocols.
definitions.paper: Do as We Do, Not as You Think: the Conformity of Large Language Models

definitions.text: A psychological experiment replicated to test an LLM's tendency to conform to group pressure by having it perform a visual discrimination task alongside confederates who give incorrect answers.
definitions.paper: Human Simulacra: Benchmarking the Personification of Large Language Models

characterization: A series of classic social psychology experiments studying how individual judgment is influenced by a majority group opinion, which inspired the design of the Trust and Doubt protocols.
